_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to analyze a finding of a compromise and develop a custom signature(s) and/or rule(s) to identify it throughout the network
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing matching and merging rules for entity resolution, including rule documentation, threshold calibration, and exception handling workflows.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI DQ domain. DQ-S-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-S-002 (SP, cosine: 0.409), RC-S-005 (RC, cosine: 0.392).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.2/10 (raw 0.622). Shared vocabulary: [rule]. DCWF-only terms [ability, analyze, compromise, custom, develop, finding] vs WIDAI-only terms [calibration, designing, documentation, entity, exception, handling] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.853 (P=0.848, R=0.858). Balanced P/R — the 21-word DCWF element and 19-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Ability to analyze a finding of a compromise and develop a custom signature(s) a" and "Skill in designing matching and merging rules for entity resolution, including r" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.430 < STS 0.622 (gap -0.192). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [ability, analyze, compromise] and [calibration, designing, documentation] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.7%. Shared tokens: [rule]. DCWF-only: [ability, analyze, compromise, custom, develop, finding]. WIDAI-only: [calibration, designing, documentation, entity, exception, handling].
